fields.name: Arvani Common
fields.description: The tongue of the old world-spanning empire whose capital was Arx, preserved a thousand years past its fall as the language of scholars, liturgy, treaties, and trade between realms. No one's cradle-tongue any longer — which is exactly why everyone educated learns it.

fields.name: Umbral
fields.description: The tongue of the Umbral Empire: clipped, exact, and built like its blackstone — few ornaments, no wasted syllables, and a proverb for every kind of strength.

fields.name: Luxeri
fields.description: The tongue of the Holy Republic of Luxen, and — history being what it is — of the natives of Inferna as well. Liquid and courtly, the language of sermons, salons, and sentences passed.

fields.name: Cinderi
fields.description: The tongue of lost Cinderus, carried into exile by the winged diaspora and still spoken in southern Luxen and across the Isles. Warm, rolling, and stubborn: every generation that keeps it is a refusal to forget.

fields.name: Ari
fields.description: The tongue of the Kingdom of Ariwn — 'Ariwn' itself means blood. Its formal registers survive unchanged for centuries, because in Ariwn, so do the speakers.

fields.name: Aythir
fields.description: The tongue of the Northlands: spare, practical, and rich beyond reason in words for ice, weather, and the moods of the dead.

fields.name: Nox'alfar
fields.description: The elven tongue of the Nox'alfar. Beautiful, precise, and unnervingly good at meaning several things at once.
